speaker:
FIRST
               
              MERCHANT
text: Therefore give out you are of Epidamium,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MERCHANT
text: Lest that your goods too soon be confiscate.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MERCHANT
text: This very day a Syracusian merchant

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MERCHANT
text: Is apprehended for arrival here

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MERCHANT
text: And, not being able to buy out his life,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MERCHANT
text: According to the statute of the town

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MERCHANT
text: Dies ere the weary sun set in the west.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MERCHANT
text: There is your money that I had to keep.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Go bear it to the Centaur, where we host,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: And stay there, Dromio, till I come to thee.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Within this hour it will be dinnertime.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Till that, I’ll view the manners of the town,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Peruse the traders, gaze upon the buildings,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: And then return and sleep within mine inn,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: For with long travel I am stiff and weary.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Get thee away.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Many a man would take you at your word

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: And go indeed, having so good a mean.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: A trusty villain, sir, that very oft,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: When I am dull with care and melancholy,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Lightens my humor with his merry jests.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: What, will you walk with me about the town

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: And then go to my inn and dine with me?

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MERCHANT
text: I am invited, sir, to certain merchants,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MERCHANT
text: Of whom I hope to make much benefit.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MERCHANT
text: I crave your pardon. Soon at five o’clock,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MERCHANT
text: Please you, I’ll meet with you upon the mart

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MERCHANT
text: And afterward consort you till bedtime.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MERCHANT
text: My present business calls me from you now.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Farewell till then. I will go lose myself

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: And wander up and down to view the city.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MERCHANT
text: Sir, I commend you to your own content.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: He that commends me to mine own content

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Commends me to the thing I cannot get.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: I to the world am like a drop of water

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: That in the ocean seeks another drop,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Who, falling there to find his fellow forth,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Unseen, inquisitive, confounds himself.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: So I, to find a mother and a brother,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: In quest of them, unhappy, lose myself.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Here comes the almanac of my true date.—

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: What now? How chance thou art returned so soon?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Returned so soon? Rather approached too late!

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: The capon burns; the pig falls from the spit;

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: The clock hath strucken twelve upon the bell;

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: My mistress made it one upon my cheek.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: She is so hot because the meat is cold;

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: The meat is cold because you come not home;

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: You come not home because you have no stomach;

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: You have no stomach, having broke your fast.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: But we that know what ’tis to fast and pray

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Are penitent for your default today.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Stop in your wind, sir. Tell me this, I pray:

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Where have you left the money that I gave you?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: O, sixpence that I had o’ Wednesday last

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: To pay the saddler for my mistress’ crupper?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: The saddler had it, sir; I kept it not.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: I am not in a sportive humor now.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Tell me, and dally not: where is the money?

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: We being strangers here, how dar’st thou trust

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: So great a charge from thine own custody?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: I pray you, jest, sir, as you sit at dinner.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: I from my mistress come to you in post;

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: If I return, I shall be post indeed,

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: For she will scour your fault upon my pate.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Methinks your maw, like mine, should be your clock,

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: And strike you home without a messenger.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Come, Dromio, come, these jests are out of season.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Reserve them till a merrier hour than this.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Where is the gold I gave in charge to thee?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: To me, sir? Why, you gave no gold to me!

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Come on, sir knave, have done your foolishness,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: And tell me how thou hast disposed thy charge.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: My charge was but to fetch you from the mart

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Home to your house, the Phoenix, sir, to dinner.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: My mistress and her sister stays for you.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Now, as I am a Christian, answer me

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: In what safe place you have bestowed my money,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Or I shall break that merry sconce of yours

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: That stands on tricks when I am undisposed.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Where is the thousand marks thou hadst of me?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: I have some marks of yours upon my pate,

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Some of my mistress’ marks upon my shoulders,

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: But not a thousand marks between you both.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: If I should pay your Worship those again,

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Perchance you will not bear them patiently.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Thy mistress’ marks? What mistress, slave, hast thou?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Your Worship’s wife, my mistress at the Phoenix,

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: She that doth fast till you come home to dinner

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: And prays that you will hie you home to dinner.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: What, wilt thou flout me thus unto my face,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Being forbid? There, take you that, sir knave.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: What mean you, sir? For God’s sake, hold your hands.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Nay, an you will not, sir, I’ll take my heels.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Upon my life, by some device or other

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: The villain is o’erraught of all my money.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: They say this town is full of cozenage,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: As nimble jugglers that deceive the eye,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Dark-working sorcerers that change the mind,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Soul-killing witches that deform the body,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Disguisèd cheaters, prating mountebanks,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: And many suchlike liberties of sin.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: If it prove so, I will be gone the sooner.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: I’ll to the Centaur to go seek this slave.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: I greatly fear my money is not safe.
